title: Tectaria rufovillosa
taxonomy: Tectaria rufovillosa belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns. This species is further classified within the genus Tectaria, which includes various species of ferns found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Tectaria rufovillosa is based on its morphological and molecular characteristics, which distinguish it from other species within the Dryopteridaceae family. Tectaria rufovillosa is a species of fern that is native to certain parts of the world, and its classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history. The classification of this species is an ongoing area of research in the field of botany.

intro: Tectaria rufovillosa, also known as the Red-veined Fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The Red-veined Fern has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Tectaria rufovillosa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
